title: New test: man page cross-ref against --help
body:
New hack/man-page-option-checker, added to 'make validate',
runs 'podman [subcommand] --help' and cross-references
against man pages in docs/source/markdown/podman*.1.md

See #5453 and #5460 for instances of the problems the
script has found.

The careful reader will find an alarming number of special-case
bypasses. These are a tradeoff I am making: to get perfect
coverage with no handwaving, it would be necessary to make
drastic changes to some man pages, and I believe those would
be counterproductive.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: Looks like `make validate` runs before `make`. Any suggestions on where to move this check? It must (obviously) run after `./bin/podman` exists.
Couldn't you just add it to validate and make it require docs?
`docs` isn't the problem; `bin/podman` is. I need a compiled executable with which to run `podman --help`.
@cevich could you PTAL at `.cirrus.yml` and see if that's an okay place to add this test? It runs in <4s, so it shouldn't be too onerous an addition to the test matrix.
LGTM, but would like a @cevich head nod

> docs isn't the problem; bin/podman is. I need a compiled executable with which to run podman --help.

Since we're building podman in the gate task anyway, and this is a really quick check, it's a fine place for this.  Also, it's intended for developers to be able to use the gate image locally, so supporting the check here is a good idea (assuming a working podman).  

Possible perl-script maintenance arguments aside, I do worry if it was a happy-accident perl was even installed.  It might be wise to explicitly list it in `contrib/gate/Dockerfile` for extra assurance.

Otherwise this LGTM
I'm a little confused. Where does `gate` fit into anything? I added the step in `testing_task`, that doesn't have anything to do with `gating_task`, does it? Other than a dependency?
Oh sorry it's probably ***me*** that was confused.  I thought I was looking at the `gate_task` in the diff, peeping again...

...okay, hmmm, not sure what I was looking at before.  Taking a second look, this may not be the best place to run this check because updating man pages could be considered work on "docs" and get caught by this in `testing_task`:

```yaml
    only_if: >-
        $CIRRUS_CHANGE_MESSAGE !=~ '.*CI:IMG.*' &&
        $CIRRUS_CHANGE_MESSAGE !=~ '.*CI:DOCS.*'
```

In other words, someone edits a man page, and sets `[CI:DOCS]` on their PR, inadvertently blocking this check :disappointed:  Reminder: That workflow is designed for only-docs related updates, where we want to skip all the other binary-related testing.

Hmmm, so maybe could this be added to the `docs_task`?  That will always run on the default VM (F31 presently), and since `setup_environment.sh` doesn't run, a stable podman (from RPM) will be available, though it won't be built from source.

If you need to test against a built podman, then adding your script into a Makefile target (new?) such that it runs during `gate_task` is probably the safest.

Unless, do you disagree about my `[CI:DOCS]` concern?

>Hmmm, so maybe could this be added to the docs_task?

That would solve the problem for `CI:DOCS` PRs but completely break every other PR: in general, a change that adds a 'podman foo --bar` option is accompanied by a corresponding addition of `--bar` to the man page, and if this test runs a prepackaged podman, without `foo --bar`, the check will fail.

When I thought about this last week, I came to the conclusion that `CI:DOCS` should/would be used for wording changes, rephrasing, but not significant additions/removals of flags from man pages. This may be an incorrect assumption: I haven't actually checked git history. 

>If you need to test against a built podman, then adding your script into a Makefile target (new?) such that it runs during gate_task is probably the safest.

I'm having trouble parsing this. Can you show me a code diff that you think would work?

> but completely break every other PR

Good point.

> but not significant additions/removals of flags from man pages

Hmmm, yes, I agree, and it's purpose is less than clear as well.

> I'm having trouble parsing this. Can you show me a code diff that you think would work?

Sure, happy to...

Maybe something like this?

```diff
diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
index fa5c3bb73..e7f7c8b98 100644
--- a/.cirrus.yml
+++ b/.cirrus.yml
@@ -136,8 +136,8 @@ gating_task:
         - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh podman |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
         - 'cd $GOSRC && ./hack/podman-commands.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
         # N/B: need 'clean' so some committed files are re-generated.
-        - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh clean podman-remote |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
-        - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh clean podman BUILDTAGS="exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper selinux seccomp" |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
+        - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh clean podman-remote check_docs |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
+        - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh clean podman check_docs BUILDTAGS="exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper selinux seccomp" |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
         - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh local-cross |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
         - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh podman-remote-darwin |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
         - '/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh podman-remote-windows |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
@@ -417,7 +417,6 @@ testing_task:
     networking_script: '${CIRRUS_WORKING_DIR}/${SCRIPT_BASE}/networking.sh'
     setup_environment_script: '$SCRIPT_BASE/setup_environment.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
     unit_test_script: '$SCRIPT_BASE/unit_test.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP}'
-    man_page_help_xref_script: './hack/man-page-option-checker'
     integration_test_script: '$SCRIPT_BASE/integration_test.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP} | ${LOGFORMAT} integration_test'
     system_test_script: '$SCRIPT_BASE/system_test.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP} | ${LOGFORMAT} system_test'
     apiv2_test_script: '$SCRIPT_BASE/apiv2_test.sh |& ${TIMESTAMP} | ${LOGFORMAT} apiv2_test'
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 6104c444c..4f21c4956 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -383,6 +383,10 @@ docdir:
 .PHONY: docs
 docs: .install.md2man docdir $(MANPAGES) ## Generate documentation
 
+.PHONY: check_docs
+check_docs: docs
+       ./hack/man-page-option-checker
+
 install-podman-remote-%-docs: podman-remote docs $(MANPAGES)
        rm -rf docs/build/remote
        mkdir -p docs/build/remote
```
Thanks, that looks promising. I'll give it a try once CI is fixed. (Not with podman-remote, though, no sense bothering with that).
I fixed a bug in the man-page handling, and rebased. Tests are green.
I have spent about 15 minutes in my life working on Perl, but it LGTM
/lgtm
/approve
/approve
/approve
